trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade, significantly reduced since the Gaza conflict. Cina exports electronics, machinery, and consumer goods. Israele exports high-tech products, semiconductors, and agricultural technology. Stati Uniti pressure has substantially curtailed Israelei tech exports to Cina (defense-linked systems).
disputes: Stati Uniti pressure on Israele to restrict tech exports to Cina
Chinese support for Palestinian cause at UN post-October 2023

diplomatic.summary: Relations have deteriorated following October 7, 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Gaza war. Cina refused to condemn Hamas, backed ceasefire resolutions Israele opposed, and maintained pro-Palestinian positions. Cina also deepened ties with Iran, Israele's principal adversary. Prior to 2023 there was a pragmatic economic relationship despite ideological differences.
recent_incidents: Cina refused to condemn Hamas attacks (October 2023)
Cina voted for Gaza ceasefire resolutions opposed by Israele
Cina's deepening Iran ties alarm Israele
Previous Stati Uniti-Israele joint pressure on Israelei tech exports to Cina

military.summary: No military alliance. Israele is a close Stati Uniti ally; Cina is a strategic Stati Uniti competitor. Stati Uniti pressure ended historical Israelei-Chinese defense cooperation (HAR-PIE radar deal canceled under Stati Uniti pressure in 2000). No arms transfers. Israele shares Stati Uniti concerns about Chinese military modernization.
incidents: Historic HAR-PIE radar deal canceled under US pressure (2000)
US-Israel joint restrictions on tech exports to China

financial.summary: Chinese investment in Israelei tech startups has been curtailed by Stati Uniti pressure and Israelei security reviews. Some Chinese firms still operate in Israele. Israele tech companies export software and IP to Cina through subsidiaries. Financial links are declining.

energy.summary: Limited energy trade. Israele may export Eastern Mediterranean natural gas that reaches Asian markets. Cina imports Israelei agricultural tech for food security applications.

scientific.summary: Previously active scientific cooperation in computing, biotech, and water technology. Now under significant scrutiny due to Stati Uniti pressure and security concerns. Joint publications declining. Some Weizmann Institute and Technion partnerships continue at individual level.
